title: PostgreSQL Work Claims, SKIP LOCKED, Leases, and Fencing · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Short Claim Transaction
summary: Multiple instances poll one source, row locks span network calls, expired workers commit late results, leases renew silently after ownership loss, and queue fairness is unknown. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go short claim transaction increment in multi-instance fenced fetch-claim protocol. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Claim a bounded due batch with ordered FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED work in a short transaction and release locks before network I/O.
Make manual, policy, repeated-failure, deletion, and incident pauses explicit, authorized, observable, and safely resumable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
